zh.lifeSummary: Demenge Colin於1550年出生。他和Marguerite BERNIER至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Demenge Colin于1550年出生。他和Marguerite BERNIER至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Colin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Alfredo, Francisco, Jesus, Ricardo, Alberto, Angel, Arturo, Eduardo, Esteban, Faustino.  French Alexandre, Andre, Armand, Georges, Henri, Jean Claude, Jean-Michel, Laurette, Lucien, Marcel, Patrice, Pierre.
French, Walloon, and Breton: from a French shortened pet form of the personal name   Nicolas   (see   Nicholas  ). This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
English: from the personal name Colin, a diminutive of Col, itself a pet form of Nicholas .

variants.name: Collin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Leonide, Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Firmin, Francois, Ghislaine, Normand.
English, French, Walloon, and Breton: variant of   Colin  . This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
German: variant of   Kollin   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Collins
meanings: English: variant of Colin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

Irish and Manx: shortened Anglicized from Ó Coileáin, compare Cullen , or in Man or west Ulster shortened from Mac Coileáin, compare McQuillan and McCallion . The genitival -s is a local addition to Collin and variants after the surname was Anglicized.
Americanized form of French   Colin   or   Collin   and also   Collette  .

variants.name: Collinson
meanings: English (northern England and Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Colin + son.

variants.name: Collen
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Colin  .

variants.name: Colan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Anselmo, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Marcos, Margarita, Pablo, Roberto.
English: probably an altered form of   Colin  .
Hispanic (mainly Peru; Colán): unexplained.

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Angelito, Emilio, Fernando, Francisco, Manuel, Mario, Ricardo, Rodolfo, Ruben, Alfredo, Angel.  French Luc, Pierre, Andre, Georges, Jacques, Marcellin, Yves, Adrien, Alain, Camille, Celina, Dominique.

Spanish (Nicolás), French, Walloon, Breton, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the personal name Nicolás (Spanish), Nicolas (French, Breton), from Greek Nikolaos (see Nicholas ). In France, this surname is most common in Brittany.
English (southern England and Glamorgan): variant of   Nicholas  .

nameMeanings.name: Demenge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre.
French: from a regional variant of the personal name   Dominique  .

variants.name: Demanche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Cecile, Marcel, Michel, Normand.
French: variant of   Demange   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Dominique
meanings: Feminine form of   Dominic  , from a French form that is used as both a girl's and a boy's name.
